executive_takeaway: Do not install or run a live AI gateway yet. Build a local egress-governance contract first, keep the SQLite CEO ledger authoritative, and use LiteLLM as the best future local-first model/MCP gateway candidate.

candidates.current_signal: Matches existing CEO ledger and chain-integrity model.
candidates.main_risk: Does not perform live gateway enforcement by itself.

candidates.current_signal: Open-source AI Gateway for 100+ LLM providers with MCP access controls by key, team, and organization.
candidates.main_risk: Needs the lab ledger for browser, API, public action, account, wallet, and payment policy.

candidates.current_signal: Open-source AI Gateway with routing, fallbacks, load balancing, retries, guardrails, multimodal support, and agentic workflow integrations.
candidates.main_risk: Live deployment adds dependency and credential surface.

candidates.current_signal: Unified API, LLM, MCP, and agent-to-agent governance from a mature gateway platform.
candidates.main_risk: Enterprise complexity and not a next local step.

candidates.current_signal: Unified registry, step-level observability, RBAC, policy enforcement, governed MCP access, retries, timeouts, and fallbacks.
candidates.main_risk: Commercial/platform scope is too broad before local contracts exist.

candidates.current_signal: Architecture argument for governing LLM, MCP, and API traffic through distributed control plane.
candidates.main_risk: Vendor-framed source needs independent verification before adoption.

candidates.current_signal: Centralized runtime guardrails across frameworks using configurable evaluators.
candidates.main_risk: Small project and not a full egress gateway.
